v4.0
Lingua Omnibus · Technical Specification

Program in
any human language.

A universal programming language — multilingual, multi-architecture, with integrated databases and native hybrid-quantum computing. Created by Claudiu Groza (Vemeth).

🌍 30+ Natural Languages ⚛️ Hybrid-Quantum 🗄️ Native LinguaDB ⚡ Adaptive JIT 🤖 AI-Extensible 🔒 Linear Types
30+
Natural Languages
5
Hardware Architectures
3
Compiler Layers
∞
Possibilities
01 · Philosophy

Why Lingua Omnibus?

There are approximately 700 million potential programmers who are excluded from programming because of the language barrier. All modern programming languages are, in essence, dialects of English.

Lingua Omnibus breaks this tradition. The semantics remain universal, but the syntax is the expression of your native language.

Vision: The same program, written in Romanian, Japanese, Arabic or Swahili, produces identical, optimized machine code. Language is not an obstacle — it is a bridge.
1
Universal Linguistic Accessibility

Keywords are mapped from any natural language to internal abstract tokens. The semantic core is completely isolated from language.

2
Total Hardware Portability

Native compilation for x86-64, ARM, RISC-V, PowerPC, and QPU. One source file, any target.

3
Native Persistence Without Friction

ACID database engine integrated directly in the language. No more object-relational impedance mismatch.

4
Intrinsic Adaptability

Tiered JIT, hardware adapters, context-aware specialization. Code self-optimizes based on the machine it runs on.

5
Verifiable Correctness

Linear types for qubits (no-cloning theorem), static type system with inference, formally verifiable LIR.

02 · Architecture

Three layers. One semantics.

Strict separation allows the same semantic code to compile for any hardware target, while the frontend is fully replaceable without affecting the rest of the pipeline.

Layer 1 · Frontend
Multilingual Surface — Per-Language Parsers
🇷🇴 Română🇬🇧 English 🇪🇸 Español🇫🇷 Français 🇩🇪 Deutsch🇯🇵 日本語 🇸🇦 العربية🇮🇳 हिन्दी 🇨🇳 中文🇷🇺 Русский 🇵🇹 Português🇰🇷 한국어 🇮🇹 Italiano🇵🇱 Polski + 16 more

Each parser produces the same unified AST. Automatic language detection or explicit #lingua: XX directive.

↓
Layer 2 · Semantic Core
Lingua Intermediate Representation (LIR)
Unified AST Type Inference Constant Folding Inlining Devirtualization Formal Verification

Statically typed, language-and-architecture agnostic. Based on an MLIR subset with quantum and DB dialects.

↓
Layer 3 · Backend
Multi-Target Execution
x86_64 · AVX-512 ARM · SVE2 RISC-V · Vector 1.0 PowerPC · VSX QPU · OpenQASM 3.0 WebAssembly
03 · Syntax

Same program. Six languages.

The compiler auto-detects the language from tokens or via the #lingua: RO directive. Each language has a lexical mapping file.

Fibonacci(n) — parallel translations · identical semantics, compiled identically
🇷🇴 Română
funcție fib(n: Întreg) returnează Întreg:
    dacă n ≤ 1 atunci:
        returnează n
    altfel:
        returnează fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
🇬🇧 English
function fib(n: Integer) returns Integer:
    if n ≤ 1 then:
        return n
    else:
        return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
🇫🇷 Français
fonction fib(n: Entier) retourne Entier:
    si n ≤ 1 alors:
        retourne n
    sinon:
        retourne fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
🇩🇪 Deutsch
Funktion fib(n: Ganzzahl) gibt zurück Ganzzahl:
    wenn n ≤ 1 dann:
        zurückgeben n
    sonst:
        zurückgeben fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
🇪🇸 Español
función fib(n: Entero) devuelve Entero:
    si n ≤ 1 entonces:
        devuelve n
    si no:
        devuelve fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
🇯🇵 日本語
関数 fib(n: 整数) 返す 整数:
    もし n ≤ 1 ならば:
        返す n
    そうでなければ:
        返す fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)
04 · Type System

Static. Safe. Universal.

All type names are translatable. The compiler knows Integer, Entier, Ganzzahl, 整数, and Întreg as the same primitive.

🔢

Primitives

Integer/Entier/整数 · Float · Bool · Text/String · Byte · Unit

📦

Composite

Array<T> · Map<K,V> · Tuple<A,B> · Option<T> · Result<T,E> · structs

⚛️

Linear (Quantum)

Qubit (non-clonable) · QReg<N> · QCircuit<I,O> · QState<N>

v4.0 New: Affine Types & Algebraic Effects

// Effects declared explicitly in the function signature
function read_and_save(path: String) returns Result<Unit, Error>
    with_effects [IO, Database]:
    var content = read_file(path)?   // IO effect
    insert into Log(content: content) // Database effect
    return Ok()
05 · Hybrid-Quantum Computing

Qubits are first-class citizens.

L.O. treats qubits as linear resources verified at compile time. quantum { ... } blocks are compiled into OpenQASM 3.0 circuits and can run on local simulators or cloud QPUs (IBM, IonQ, Rigetti, Azure Quantum).

Bell Circuit — Multilingual Examples

#lingua: EN  — maximally entangled Bell pair

function bell_circuit() returns Tuple<Bool, Bool>:
    quantum:
        qubit q0, q1
        hadamard(q0)           // superposition
        cnot(q0, q1)           // entanglement
        r0 = measure q0
        r1 = measure q1
    return (r0, r1)

// Possible results: (0,0) or (1,1) — never (0,1) or (1,0)
Compile-time guarantee: The Qubit type is linear — the compiler rejects any attempt to copy or re-measure a qubit, respecting the no-cloning theorem.
⚛ Bell Circuit Visualizer · 2 qubits
q₀ ─
H
●
M
→ b₀
q₁ ─
⊕
M
→ b₁
Probability distribution:
|00⟩ ──────── 50%
|01⟩ ── 0%
|10⟩ ── 0%
|11⟩ ──────── 50%

Quantum Hardware Backends

ProviderQubitsProtocol
IBM Quantum127–433OpenQASM 3.0
IonQ32–64Native Gates
Rigetti80Quil + QCS
Azure QuantumvariableQ# interop
Local Simulator≤ 32Built-in
06 · LinguaDB

Databases as native syntax.

LinguaDB is an ACID engine integrated directly in the compiler. Schemas are statically verified at compile time, not at runtime. Supports relational models, JSON documents, and vector indexes for semantic search.

Relational Operations

#lingua: EN

create table Employees if_not_exists (
    id      Integer   primary_key auto_increment,
    name    Text(100) not_null,
    salary  Float,
    dept    Text,
    created Time      default NOW()
)

insert into Employees (name, salary, dept)
    values ("Ana Ionescu", 7500.0, "Engineering")

var eng_team =
    select name, salary from Employees
    where dept = "Engineering" and salary > 5000
    order by salary descending

for emp in eng_team:
    print(emp.name, " earns ", emp.salary)

Vector Semantic Search

// Vector indexes for AI embeddings
create table Documents (
    id      Integer primary_key,
    title   Text,
    content Text,
    vector  Float[1536]   // OpenAI embedding
)
create index idx_vec type HNSW on Documents(vector)
    with distance = "cosine"

function find_similar(query: Text, top_k: Integer):
    var emb = embedding(query)
    return
        select title, content,
               cosine_sim(vector, emb) as score
        from Documents
        order by score descending
        limit top_k
Schema verified at compile time: If you access a non-existent field, the compiler produces an error — not a runtime crash. LinguaDB completely eliminates SQL injection through implicit parameterization.
07 · Interactive Demo

Choose a language. Read the code.

The examples below show the same complete program — user registration with validation and persistence — in six natural languages.

visitor_app.lo
▶ Simulated Output
loc compile visitor_app.lo --target=x86_64
✓ Language detected: English (EN)
✓ Schema 'Visitors' statically validated
✓ LIR compilation complete (0 errors, 0 warnings)
✓ Backend x86_64 (AVX-512 enabled)
Name: Ana Ionescu
Age: 25
Welcome, Ana Ionescu!
✓ Saved to database.
Compile time: 47ms
Binary size: 18KB
08 · Comparison

L.O. vs. existing alternatives.

FeatureLingua OmnibusPythonRustJuliaQ#
Native multilingual syntax✓ 30+ languages✗✗✗✗
Integrated quantum computing✓ Native~ Qiskit ext.~ external~ Yao.jl✓ Native
Native DB without ORM✓ LinguaDB✗✗✗✗
Linear types for qubits✓✗~ Affine✗✓
Native multi-arch compilation✓ 5 arch✗ Interpreted✓~ LLVM~ QIR
Adaptive JIT✓~ PyPy✗✓✗
LIR formal verification✓✗~✗~
Static schema verification✓✗~ SQLx✗✗
09 · Ecosystem

Tools built for every language.

⚙️

loc — Main Compiler

Multilingual frontend + LIR + hardware backend. Automatic language detection, error reports in the source language, cross-compilation support.

loc build main.lo --target=arm64
loc build main.lo --target=qpu:ibm
📦

lopm — Package Manager

Multilingual registry. Packages can be written in any natural language and consumed in any other. Semantic resolution, not textual.

lopm add advanced-math
lopm search "linear algebra"
🎨

lofmt — Formatter

Code formatting that respects the conventions of the chosen natural language. Diacritics support, RTL (Arabic, Hebrew), non-Latin scripts.

lofmt --lingua=AR file.lo
lofmt --detect-lingua *.lo
🔌

lolsp — Language Server

Autocompletion, diagnostics, hover, rename — all in the natural language of the source. Compatible with VSCode, Neovim, JetBrains, Emacs.

lolsp --port 7777 &
# Automatic LSP integration
📚

lodoc — Doc Generator

Generates HTML/Markdown documentation in the code's source language. Automatically translates comments into other languages on demand.

lodoc --translate-to=FR project/
lodoc --format=html src/
🔬

lotest — Test Framework

Unit, integration, and property-based tests in natural language. Multilingual reports.

lotest run --parallel 8
lotest coverage --format=html
10 · Roadmap

What's next for L.O.

Phase 1 · 2025 Q1–Q2 ✓
Foundations — Specification & Prototype
Formal LIR specification, prototype parser for RO/EN/ES/FR/JA, basic relational LinguaDB, static type system.
Phase 2 · 2025 Q3–Q4
Full Compiler & x86_64 Backend
End-to-end compiler pipeline, x86_64 backend with AVX-512, tiered JIT, functional loc and lopm, 10 language support.
Phase 3 · 2026 Q1–Q2
Multi-Architecture & Quantum
ARM/RISC-V/PowerPC backends, IBM+IonQ QPU integration, LinguaDB vector indexes, algebraic effects system, Language Server.
Phase 4 · 2026 Q3–Q4
Ecosystem & Community
30+ natural languages, public package registry, IDE plugins, interactive documentation, first open-source projects in L.O.
Phase 5 · 2027+
Formal Verification & AI-Assisted
Integrated LIR prover, LLM-assisted code generation in any natural language, native RTL and ideographic script support.

Planned Languages v4.1+

Through community contributions, L.O. targets 50 natural languages by 2027:

🇸🇦 Arabic ✓ 🇨🇳 Simplified Chinese ✓ 🇹🇼 Traditional Chinese 🇮🇩 Indonesian 🇧🇷 Portuguese ✓ 🇷🇺 Russian ✓ 🇳🇬 Swahili 🇹🇷 Turkish 🇻🇳 Vietnamese 🇬🇷 Greek 🇮🇱 Hebrew (RTL) 🇹🇭 Thai 🇺🇦 Ukrainian 🇳🇱 Dutch 🇸🇪 Swedish 🇮🇹 Italian ✓ 🇵🇱 Polish

Contribute to L.O.

Adding a new dialect requires a single JSON lexical mapping file and regression tests. No compiler changes needed.

{
  "lingua": "SW",
  "name": "Kiswahili",
  "keywords": {
    "KW_IF":       "kama",
    "KW_ELSE":     "au",
    "KW_FUNCTION": "kazi",
    "KW_RETURNS":  "rudisha",
    "KW_VAR":      "kigezo",
    "KW_FOR":      "kwa"
  }
}
Looking for collaborators for new backends (GPU CUDA, WebGPU), uncovered natural languages, and formal LIR verification. Apache 2.0 license.
11 · References

Theoretical foundations.

LLVM / MLIR

Compiler infrastructure with extensible intermediate representations. LIR is an MLIR dialect with quantum and DB extensions.

CompCert

Formally verified C compiler, proving that compilers with formal verification are practically possible.

Synaphe / Quipper

Quantum languages with linear types — the basis for treating qubits as non-clonable resources in L.O.

SELF / HotSpot JVM

Prototypes for adaptive JIT optimizations at runtime, inline caching and type-based specialization.

Esquemático

Scheme dialect with Spanish keywords — proof that languages with multilingual syntax are viable.

OpenQASM 3.0

The de facto standard for quantum circuits, chosen as the output format for L.O.'s quantum backend.

Lingua Omnibus

Technical Documentation v4.0 · April 2026 · Apache 2.0 License

Created by Claudiu Groza (Vemeth) · Open project for international collaboration

"Every human mind deserves to program in its own language."
